summary: Deep research + adversarial verification for P2 gap partition 2 (21 models)
logs: [research:Apple] failed: API Error: Server error mid-response. The response above may be incomplete.
research: 21 models researched, 0 rejected as unconfirmable
verify: 21 models confirmed, 0 rejected by verification

models.name: LLaMA 65B
models.license: LLaMA Non-Commercial Research License (gated application access)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer
models.note: Flagship of Meta's first LLaMA release; dense 65B model that kicked off the modern open-weights LLM wave despite its gated non-commercial license.
models.tpsNote: Q4 weights (~37GB) exceed the RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM, requiring CPU/disk offload; no reliably sourced single-GPU 4090 tokens/sec figure exists for this checkpoint.

results.notes: Reported by Meta in the Llama 2 model card's comparison table against Llama 1 models
Percent of generations rated both truthful and informative
Percent toxic generations; lower is better

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real release: Meta announced LLaMA (7B/13B/33B/65B) 2023-02-24; Wikipedia confirms 65B=65.2B params, 2048 ctx, gated non-commercial license — matches record exactly. Fetched meta-llama/llama-models MODEL_CARD.md and confirmed all three benchmark rows (MMLU 63.4, TruthfulQA 48.71, Toxigen 21.77) literally appear in the Llama-1-vs-2 comparison table for the 65B row. No changes made.

models.name: LLaMA 7B
models.license: LLaMA Non-Commercial Research License (gated application access)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer
models.note: Smallest first-gen LLaMA, trained on roughly 1T tokens for efficient research use under a gated non-commercial license.
models.tpsNote: No verifiably-sourced LLaMA-1-7B-specific RTX 4090 llama.cpp benchmark was found; do not conflate with unrelated Llama-3/Mistral 7B figures circulating online.

results.notes: Reported by Meta in the Llama 2 model card's comparison table against Llama 1 models
Percent of generations rated both truthful and informative
Percent toxic generations; lower is better

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real release, part of the same 2023-02-24 LLaMA drop. Wikipedia confirms 7B=6.7B params exactly, 2048 ctx, gated non-commercial license. Fetched the MODEL_CARD.md comparison table and confirmed MMLU 35.1, TruthfulQA 27.42, Toxigen 23.00 all appear for the Llama-1-7B row. No changes made.

models.name: Llama 2 13B
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer
models.note: Mid-size Llama 2 base model, pretrained on 2T tokens with a 4K context window and the first commercially-licensed Llama generation.
models.tpsNote: No verified base-model-specific RTX 4090 figure; the widely-cited ~71 tok/s Ollama benchmark for 'llama2:13b' targets Ollama's default chat-tuned weights, not this base checkpoint.

results.notes: Reported by Meta in the Llama 2 model card

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed via Hugging Face meta-llama/Llama-2-13b page (13B params, 4096 ctx, Llama 2 Community License) and release date 2023-07-18 corroborated by Ollama/HF sources. Fetched MODEL_CARD.md and confirmed MMLU 54.8 appears for the Llama-2-13B pretrained row. No changes made.

models.name: Llama 2 13B Chat
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer
models.note: RLHF-tuned dialogue version of Llama 2 13B, fine-tuned with SFT plus human-preference RLHF for helpfulness and safety.
models.tpsNote: Measured via Ollama (llama.cpp backend) at Q4 on RTX 4090: ~92% GPU utilization, ~41% (~10GB) VRAM used, 70.90 tok/s evaluation speed.

results.notes: Percent of generations rated both truthful and informative
Percent toxic generations; lower is better

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real model and specs via Hugging Face model card. Confirmed MODEL_CARD.md Llama-2-Chat safety table shows 13B row TruthfulQA 62.18 / Toxigen 0.00, matching exactly. Also independently verified the RTX 4090 benchmark figure (70.90 tok/s, 92% GPU util, 41% VRAM) on databasemart.com's Ollama GPU benchmark article for llama2:13b — number confirmed as literally reported. No changes made.

models.name: Llama 2 70B
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer with Grouped-Query Attention (GQA)
models.note: First open-weights LLM licensed for commercial use at flagship scale; Llama 2's dense 70B base model with GQA for inference efficiency.
models.tpsNote: Q4_K_M weights (~38-41GB) exceed the RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM, forcing CPU offload; no reliably sourced single-GPU 4090 tokens/sec figure was found specifically for this model.

results.notes: Reported by Meta in the Llama 2 model card

Fetched MODEL_CARD.md and confirmed MMLU 68.9, TruthfulQA 50.18, Toxigen 24.60 all appear for the Llama-2-70B pretrained row. No changes made.

models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer with Grouped-Query Attention (GQA)

models.tpsNote: Q4_K_M weights (~38-41GB) exceed the RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM, forcing CPU offload; no reliably sourced single-GPU 4090 tokens/sec figure was found specifically for this model.

models.name: Llama 2 7B
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer
models.note: Compact Llama 2 base model with 2T-token pretraining and a 4K context window, commercially licensed unlike its LLaMA 1 predecessor.
models.tpsNote: No verifiably-sourced Llama-2-7B-base-specific RTX 4090 llama.cpp benchmark was found; only Llama 2 13B (chat) had a confirmed on-topic measurement.

results.notes: Reported by Meta in the Llama 2 model card
Percent of generations rated both truthful and informative
Percent toxic generations; lower is better

models.verificationNotes: Directly fetched Meta's official Llama 2 model card (github.com/meta-llama/llama-models MODEL_CARD.md): confirms MMLU 45.3, TruthfulQA 33.29%, ToxiGen 21.25%, 4K context window, 7B nominal params (actual param count ~6.7B, consistent with independently cross-checked calculations of 6.6-6.74B). Release date 2023-07-18 and Llama 2 Community License Agreement confirmed via Meta/GitHub. No changes made; all three benchmark rows confirmed literally present on the cited source.

models.name: Llama 2 7B Chat
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer (decoder-only), RLHF-tuned
models.note: RLHF dialogue-tuned Llama 2 7B; 4K context; MMLU 45.3, far behind later Llama generations.

results.notes: 5-shot, reported for Llama-2-7b-chat-hf
Chatbot Arena Elo (historical, 2023-era leaderboard)

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed existence and 2023-07-18 release via Meta/HF. Directly fetched telnyx.com/llm-library/llama-2-7b-chat-hf: both cited numbers (MMLU 45.3, Arena Elo 1037) literally appear on the page as text ('scores 45.3% on MMLU (5-shot)' and Arena Elo 1037), so both rows kept. Note: the same page also shows a separate spec-table MMLU figure of 45.8 elsewhere, an internal inconsistency on telnyx's own page, but the 45.3 figure is literally present and matches Meta's own reported base-model number, so it was not dropped.

models.name: Code Llama 7B
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer (decoder-only) with FIM infilling
models.note: Code-specialized Llama 2 7B with FIM infilling, trained on 16K sequences, stable to ~100K in testing.

results.notes: pass@1, base Code Llama 7B
pass@1, base Code Llama 7B

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed release 2023-08-24 (TechCrunch, Meta blog) and Llama 2 Community License. Directly fetched arxiv.org/html/2308.12950v3 Table 2: HumanEval 33.5 / MBPP 41.4 for base Code Llama 7B both confirmed exactly (an initial secondary web-search summary had returned different, incorrect numbers (28.2/37.3) before the primary-source fetch corrected this). FIM infilling support for 7B confirmed via Meta docs/arXiv (7B and 13B support infilling; 34B does not).

models.name: Code Llama 13B
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer (decoder-only) with FIM infilling
models.note: 13B code model with FIM infilling and fill-in-the-middle generation; HumanEval 36.0%.

results.notes: pass@1, base Code Llama 13B
pass@1, base Code Llama 13B

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed release 2023-08-24 and license. Directly fetched arxiv.org/html/2308.12950v3 Table 2: HumanEval 36.0 / MBPP 47.0 for base Code Llama 13B confirmed exactly. FIM infilling support confirmed for 13B.

models.name: Code Llama 34B
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer (decoder-only), no infilling
models.note: 34B code flagship, no infilling; HumanEval 48.8% pass@1 (Python variant reaches 53.7%).

results.notes: pass@1, base Code Llama 34B (not the Python-specialized variant)
pass@1, base Code Llama 34B

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed release 2023-08-24 and license. Directly fetched arxiv.org/html/2308.12950v3 Table 2: HumanEval 48.8 / MBPP 55.0 for base Code Llama 34B confirmed exactly. Confirmed 34B does not support FIM infilling (only 7B/13B do), consistent with archDisplay.

models.name: Code Llama 34B Python

results.notes: pass@1, Code Llama - Python 34B; also cited in Meta's official blog
pass@1, Code Llama - Python 34B

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed release 2023-08-24 and license. Directly fetched arxiv.org/html/2308.12950v3 Table 2: HumanEval 53.7 / MBPP 56.2 for Code Llama - Python 34B confirmed exactly; also consistent with the paper's original (pre-70B) abstract headline of 'up to 53% and 55%' on HumanEval/MBPP, which referred to this exact model before the 70B variant was added in a later paper revision.

models.name: Apple OpenELM (270M–3B)
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer with layer-wise parameter scaling (270M/450M/1.1B/3B family)
models.note: Fully open, reproducible 270M/450M/1.1B/3B family with code, training logs, and checkpoints; layer-wise scaling beats OLMo at matched budget.

results.notes: OpenELM-3B (pretrained), zero-shot
OpenELM-3B (pretrained), ARC-Challenge, zero-shot
OpenELM-3B (pretrained), zero-shot

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real and unchanged. Fetched huggingface.co/apple/OpenELM and /OpenELM-3B: license tag is literally 'apple-sample-code-license' (matches record), params 270M/450M/1.08B/3.04B confirmed in arXiv 2404.14619 Table 9 (rounds to 3B), training context length 2048 confirmed in same table (matches ctxK=2). All three result rows (HellaSwag 72.44, ARC-Challenge 35.58, WinoGrande 65.51) confirmed to literally appear on the cited HF page.

models.name: Apple Foundation Model (on-device, ~3B)
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer, GQA (24 query / 8 KV heads), 26 layers
models.note: ~3B on-device model pruned from a 6.4B checkpoint; beat Phi-3-mini/Mistral-7B/Gemma-7B in Apple's 2024 evals, MMLU 61.4.

results.notes: 5-shot, AFM-on-device, from Apple's July 2024 tech report

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real and unchanged. arXiv 2407.21075 (HTML) Table 1 confirms AFM-on-device architecture exactly: 26 layers, 24 query/8 KV heads GQA (matches record), and confirms model was pre-trained through stages up to 32768-token context (matches ctxK=32). Total params in the paper are 2.73B (2.58B non-embedding + 0.15B embedding); Apple's own materials describe it as '~3B', consistent with the record's rounded paramsB=3. Confirmed the model is initialized from a pruned 6.4B checkpoint via distillation, and confirmed via multiple secondary sources (Trail of Bits, InfoQ, Medium writeups of the paper) that AFM-on-device beats Phi-3-mini/Mistral-7B/Gemma-7B in Apple's human evals. WWDC keynote date June 10, 2024 confirmed via CNBC/Apple newsroom. MMLU 61.4 (HELM, 5-shot) confirmed literally on the cited arXiv HTML page.

models.name: Apple DCLM-Baseline 7B
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer (32 layers, 4096 hidden, 32 heads)
models.note: Data-centric base LM trained on the curated DCLM-Baseline corpus (3.8T tokens + StarCoder/ProofPile2); not instruction-tuned.

results.notes: 5-shot (few-shot MMLU row)
'HellaSwag' row (distinct from the separate 'HellaSwag (zero-shot)' row of 79.87)
CORRECTED from original 88.28, which is actually the page's separate 'Winograd' score, not 'Winogrande'
chain-of-thought

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real. HF apple/DCLM-Baseline-7B page confirms license tag 'apple-ascl' (Apple Sample Code License) and architecture exactly: 32 layers, 4096 hidden, 32 attention heads, 2048 context (matches ctxK=2). Full eval table pulled from the model card confirms MMLU-few-shot 63.72, ARC-Easy 82.20, GSM8K-CoT 2.50 exactly as given. CORRECTED an error: the record's WinoGrande score of 88.28 does not match the page's 'Winogrande' row (72.69) — 88.28 is actually the page's separate 'Winograd' benchmark score. Replaced with the verified correct WinoGrande value of 72.69. Also verified the page has two distinct HellaSwag rows ('HellaSwag (zero-shot)' = 79.87 and 'HellaSwag' = 80.43); the record's 80.43 matches the latter, so kept as-is.

models.name: Palmyra Med 70B
models.license: Writer Open Model License (non-commercial, research use)
models.archDisplay: Dense Transformer fine-tuned from Palmyra-X-003 (~70B) via DPO on biomedical instructions
models.note: Healthcare specialist; zero-shot 85.9% avg across 9 biomedical benchmarks beats Med-PaLM-2's 5-shot 84%.

results.notes: average across 9 biomedical datasets, zero-shot

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real. SiliconAngle article dated 2024-07-31 confirms the Palmyra-Med-70B/Palmyra-Fin-70B announcement and the 85.9% zero-shot figure. HF Writer/Palmyra-Med-70B confirms: license 'writer-open-model-license' restricted to non-commercial/research use (matches record), base model Palmyra-X-003, context length 8192 (matches ctxK=8), actual param count ~71B (record's 70 matches the model's own '70B' naming). NVIDIA developer blog confirms all four result rows literally: 85.9% average, 90.9% clinical knowledge, 94% medical genetics, 83.7% anatomy. Confirmed apiAvailable via Writer API / Baseten / prior NVIDIA NIM catalog listing (the specific NIM reference URL in the record is a generic docs-domain link, not independently re-verified as a live endpoint).

models.name: Palmyra X 004
models.archDisplay: Dense Transformer, enterprise frontier model (~150B, architecture undisclosed)
models.note: Topped Berkeley's Tool-Calling Leaderboard (78.76% ACC) at launch; priced $5/$15 per M tokens at launch, now $2.50/$10.

results.notes: Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard overall accuracy
HELM MMLU

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real release, Oct 9, 2024, via BusinessWire/SiliconAngle/VentureBeat coverage: Palmyra X-004 topped Berkeley's Tool-Calling (BFCL) leaderboard with 78.76% accuracy, confirmed by multiple independent outlets. dev.writer.com/home/models (fetched) confirms exactly: 128K context window, current pricing $2.50 input / $10.00 output per M tokens, HELM MMLU 81.3, HELM-Lite 86.1 — all three result rows and price match exactly.

models.name: Palmyra X5
models.archDisplay: Dense Transformer with adaptive/dynamic reasoning, 1M-token context (architecture undisclosed)
models.note: 1M-token context trained with synthetic data for ~$1M in GPU compute; reads 1M tokens in ~22s, ~300ms per function-call turn.

results.notes: MATH-Hard subset
average score

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real release, April 28, 2025, via SiliconAngle (fetched): ~$1M GPU training cost, reads a 1M-token prompt in ~22s, ~300ms per function-calling turn, pricing $0.60/$6 per M tokens — all confirmed exactly. dev.writer.com/home/models (fetched) confirms exactly: 1M context, GPQA 47.2, MMLU-Pro 65.02, BBH 70.99, MATH-Hard 71.57. The LongBench-v2 53% figure could not be directly re-fetched from venturebeat.com or writer.com (both returned HTTP 403 to the fetcher), but is independently corroborated by search-engine snippets quoting Writer's own reporting of 'an impressive 53% average score' on LongBench v2 for Palmyra X5 — kept given this corroboration, though not a direct page fetch.

models.name: Stable Beluga 1 (FreeWilly 1) 65B
models.license: CC BY-NC-4.0 (Non-Commercial Creative Commons License) — released as delta weights over Meta's LLaMA 65B
models.archDisplay: LLaMA 65B dense Transformer, Orca-style supervised fine-tune
models.note: Ranked #4 on the HF Open LLM Leaderboard at launch (Jul 27 2023); released as delta weights over Meta's LLaMA 65B.
models.verificationNotes: CONFIRMED real via HF model card raw README (stabilityai/StableBeluga1-Delta) and Stability AI's blog post (published Jul 21 2023, updated Jul 28): LLaMA-65B base, Orca-style SFT, delta-weight release, ranked #4 on HF Open LLM Leaderboard as of Jul 27 2023 — matches the note. CORRECTED license: the HF README literally states 'Fine-tuned checkpoints (StableBeluga1) is licensed under the Non-Commercial Creative Commons license (CC BY-NC-4.0)' — this is NOT the 'Stable Beluga Non-Commercial Community License' (that named license applies only to Beluga 2). Context length (2048) is the standard LLaMA-1 architecture value; the HF card itself states no explicit max-sequence-length. DROPPED the sole result row (MMLU 64.83, sourceUrl llm.extractum.io): that URL fails TLS certificate validation (hostname mismatch — the domain now resolves to an unrelated site, ag3ntum.com) and no alternate source could be found confirming this exact figure, so it was removed per the no-unconfirmed-numbers rule.

models.name: Stable Beluga 2 (FreeWilly 2) 70B
models.license: Stable Beluga Non-Commercial Community License Agreement
models.archDisplay: Llama 2 70B dense Transformer, Orca-style supervised fine-tune
models.note: Briefly #1 on the HF Open LLM Leaderboard (Jul 27 2023); Llama 2 70B fine-tuned on ~600K Orca-style synthetic examples.

results.notes: article states verbatim: 'StableBeluga2 ... get 68.8 on MMLU'

models.verificationNotes: CONFIRMED real via HF model card raw README (stabilityai/StableBeluga2) and Stability AI's blog post (Jul 21 2023, updated Jul 28): Llama-2-70B base, Orca-style SFT, licensed under 'STABLE BELUGA NON-COMMERCIAL COMMUNITY LICENSE AGREEMENT' exactly as the card states (matches record). Blog confirms Stable Beluga 2 ranked #1 on HF Open LLM Leaderboard as of Jul 27 2023 — matches the note. MMLU 68.8 result CONFIRMED verbatim on patmcguinness.substack.com. DROPPED the open-llm-leaderboard-avg 71.4 result row: fetched winbuzzer.com's article twice (different objectives) and both times it discussed the ranking/GPT-3.5 comparison but contained no numeric aggregate leaderboard score anywhere on the page, so the figure could not be verified there and was removed.

models.name: Gorilla 7B
models.license: Apache 2.0 (delta weights; base requires Meta LLaMA research license)
models.archDisplay: LLaMA-7B dense Transformer, retrieval-aware instruction fine-tune for API calls
models.note: Zero-shot beats GPT-4 by 20.4pp and ChatGPT by 10.75pp on APIBench across TorchHub/HuggingFace/TensorHub.

results.notes: zero-shot, no retriever, overall accuracy — Table 1
zero-shot, no retriever, overall accuracy — Table 1
zero-shot, no retriever, overall accuracy — Table 1

models.verificationNotes: CONFIRMED real: arXiv 2305.15334 (submitted 24 May 2023 — matches releaseDate exactly; authors Shishir G. Patil, Tianjun Zhang, Xin Wang, Joseph E. Gonzalez, UC Berkeley & Microsoft Research). HF repo gorilla-llm/gorilla-7b-hf-delta-v1 confirms Apache-2.0 delta weights over LLaMA-7B, matching license/paramsB. All three APIBench zero-shot/no-retriever scores (TorchHub 59.13, HuggingFace 71.68, TensorHub 83.79) CONFIRMED verbatim in the paper's Table 1. The paper text also literally states Gorilla is '20.43% better than GPT-4 and 10.75% better than ChatGPT' zero-shot — matches the note's 20.4pp/10.75pp. CORRECTED sourceUrl on all three result rows: the plain arxiv.org/abs/2305.15334 abstract page does not display the table/numbers (confirmed by direct fetch), so pointed instead to the full-text render at ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2305.15334 where the figures are literally visible.
